title: The One With The Ultimate Fighting Champion

lines.text: Central Perk, the whole gang minus Monica is there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you think that there's a town in Missouri or some place called Sample? And ah, as you're driving into town there's-there's like a sign, and it says "You're in Sample." (He says it like urine sample.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How'd it go with Pete?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Tell us!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're not gonna believe this. Okay, so I go over...

[Two guys walk over and interrupt her. They're both names you've already heard. One's Billy Crystal. Yes, that Billy Crystal from City Slickers. The other one is Robin Williams. Yes, that Robin Williams from Mrs. Doubtfire.]

lines.speaker: Billy Crystal
lines.text: I'm sorry. Ex-excuse us. I'm sorry, it's a little crowded. Do you mind if we... (motions to the couch)

lines.speaker: Robin Williams
lines.text: Yeah, could you scooch?

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: Yeah, move over just a little bit.
Rachel, Chandler, Phoebe, and Joey all scooch over to let them sit down.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Keep on scooching.
[cut to Monica telling the gang about what happened at Pete's]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So guys, listen, I go over there, and umm...
Robin interrupts her again by complaining loudly to Billy as the camera cuts to them.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Why? Why?! What's wrong with me?!

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: I have a feelin'... I, my wife is sleeping with her gynaecologist.
The gang is now eaves dropping in on the conversation, and is shocked.

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: How do you know?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Well y'know, he's got access.

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Y'know it's that feeling you get, y'know?

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: Like when you go bowling and you know you're in somebody else's shoes?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: That's the one.
[cut back to the gang.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, so, so you went to Pete's...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Robin is speaking loudly again) I...
[cut to Billy and Robin]

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Why is this happening to me?! I don't know, maybe it's my wound.
[cut to the gang]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Forget it. (they all turn and listen to Billy and Robin)

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: So it's-it's not heeled yet?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: No-no, it's ooozing, oozing. (to Rachel) Could you pass me the cream? Is there any-Oh, there's the cream.

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: Thomas, this is gonna be hard, but I wanted it to come from me, and nobody else.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: What is it, Tim?

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: It's me, I've been sleeping with your wife.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Billy) So you're the gynaecologist?

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: (to Joey) Hey, I'm trying to have a private conversation! Is that okay?!

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: (starting to cry) Ooh, (to Rachel) Can I have a napkin, please? Could you please hand me a napkin? (Rachel tries to grab one, but is to slow for his tastes.) Would you--Give me this thing (grabs the napkin holder from her.) all right!! Enough! (to Billy) And you are no longer my friend! We are finished! (gets up to leave) Nada!! No more! You are a bastard for doing this!! (Billy follows him) Get away from me!!

lines.speaker: Billy
lines.text: Thomas, come back here! (they both leave)
[cut to the gang, they're all stunned]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So Monica, what were you gonna tell us?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pause) I have no idea.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, come on, what was the big news Pete wanted to tell you Mon?! Or should I say Mrs. Monica Becker?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no-no oh, keep your name, don't take his name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He didn't ask me to marry him.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well then definately don't take his name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He wanted to tell me he's gonna compete is some ultimate fighting competition thingy.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: Pete?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?! What is it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know exactly. It's-it's sorta like wrestling.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (intrigued) Oh?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but without the costumes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And it's not fake, it's totally brutal.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it's two guys in a ring, and the rules are: "They're are no rules."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So you can like, bite, and pull people's hair and stuff?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, anything goes, except ah, eye gouging and fish hooking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's fish hooking?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh, what's fish hooking... (Joey sticks his finger in Ross's mouth and pulls on his cheek, y'know like when you hook a fish.) (to Joey, sarcastic) Thanks man, that would have been really hard to describe. What is that taste?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? My hands are totally clean, I just gave the duck a bath.
Chandler's office, he is just finishing a meeting with his boss.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: So thanks for the warm welcome. It's good to have you guys on my team, and I come to play. I hope you do too. Now, let's go out there and get 'em! Huh? And remember, there is no 'I' in team.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but there's two in martini, soo everybody back to my office.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: (to Chandler) You! Chuckles! What's your name?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh it's Bing, sir. I'm sorry , I was just ah...

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: No-no, I heard what you said, funny. I like funny. (Chandler starts to leave) This team is about hard work, but it's also about having fun. Good to have you aboard Bing! (smacks him on the butt, and Chandler leaves shocked.)
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is making reservations.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) That's right, Ryder. Wynona Ryder for six. (listens) Thank you. (hangs up) (to the gang) Yeah, we have the reservations.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right buddy, way to go! (smacks him on the butt)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stunned) Dude, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you! Today, my boss keep slapping my butt and he was acting like it was no big deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeesh, what'd you do about it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I didn't do anything. I didn't want to be the guy who has a problem with his boss slapping his bottom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I gotta tell ya, I think it's okay to be that guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, maybe it's like y'know, that jock thing. Y'know how football players pat each other after touchdowns. (pats Ross on the butt)
Ross throws his hands out in a "What are you doing?" gesture

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know I don't, I don't understand guys, I mean I-I would never congratulate Monica on a great stew by y'know, grabbin' her boob.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know, for a really great stew you just y'know, stick your head in between 'em.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, can we please go eat?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. What are we getting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Anything but stew.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right so, Chandler, from now on, don't give your boss a chance to get you. Y'know just ah, don't turn your back to him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, or you can teach him a lesson. Y'know? What you could do is you could rub something that really smells on your butt, all right? Then, when he goes to smack ya, his hand will smell. (thinking aloud) Now what could you rub on your butt that would smell bad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross and Monica) What if Joey were president?
Monica, Ross, Chandler, and Joey exit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, hey Rach, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you can totally say no, but umm, would it be okay with you if I set Ross up on a date?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, ah with who?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, my friend, Bonnie. She just always thought Ross was really cute, and now that you two aren't together, she asked if I could set it up, but if you're not cool with it...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh-oh, which one is Bonnie again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You remember her from my birthday party two years ago. She's yeah, like, average height, medium build, bald...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! (laughs) That's fine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Okay, good for you! (as they leave she slaps Rachel on the butt)
A Gym, Pete is training for the Ultimate Fighting Championship, with his trainer, Hoshi.

lines.speaker: Hoshi
lines.text: You are iron. You are steel! Let me ask you something, how come when I call your computer support line, I have to wait an hour and a half?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I told you, we're adding new operators all the time. Could we concentrate on my training?

lines.speaker: Hoshi
lines.text: It's just hard when I know I have e-mail I can't get!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Monica! (runs over and kisses her) Hi honey.

lines.speaker: Hoshi
lines.text: All right, on the table. (Pete gets on the table for his rubdown)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, umm, so listen umm, my friends were telling me a little about this ah, ultimate fighting thing and it, well it sounds really dangerous. I-I don't want you to get hurt, 'cause I kinda like you.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, believe me, I don't want to get hurt either. I'm being smart about this. See these guys? They're the best trainers in the world, and Hoshi here used to be a paid assassin. (Hoshi yells at him in Chinese) A house painter! He used to be a house painter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Promise me you'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I promise.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, are we still on for tonight?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, good, 'cause umm, well maybe we could have a little workout of our own...

lines.speaker: Hoshi
lines.text: No! No boom-boom before big fight!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How 'bout just a boom?
Central Perk, Phoebe is there with Bonnie, as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Well that was depressing, I think I just bought a soft pretzel from one of the kids from Fame. Ready to go to the movies?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um-hmm. Oh wait! This is Bonnie. (who has hair by the way)

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is Bonnie? (to Phoebe) This is Bonnie? (to Bonnie) You're Bonnie?

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: I can show you an ID if you want?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I'm sorry, you look a lot different from the last time I-I saw you.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Oh yeah, well I just started wearing bras again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that must be it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Bonnie) Well I hope you have fun tonight.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Thanks! You too.
Phoebe starts to leave, Rachel slowly follows, shocked about how good Bonnie looks now.
[cut to outside of Central Perk]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Phoebe) You said she was bald.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, she was bald, she's not now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How could you not tell me that she has hair?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, I hardly ever say that about people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looks in the window) Ohh, well, this is just perfect!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I'm sorry, I thought you said it was okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I said what was okay when I thought she was some weird bald chick. I mean, y'know, that girl has hair got all over head!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, maybe it won't work out. Maybe Ross won't like her personality.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why, does she have a bad personality?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, Bonnie's the best!
Chandler's office, Chandler is bent over getting some water as his boss approaches.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Bing! (Chandler stands bolt upright and turns around to face him.) Read your Computech proposal, a real homerun. (He goes to slap his butt, but Chandler slides over making him miss.) Ooh. Barely got ya that time, get over here. Come on. (Chandler goes over) Wham! (slaps him on the butt) Good one. That was a good one. (to a couple of Chandler's co-workers) Keep at it team. (goes into his office)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to his co-workers) What is with him?

lines.speaker: Phil
lines.text: With him? You're is favourite, you're his guy!

lines.speaker: Stevens
lines.text: We never get smacked.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's not true, he-he smacked you once.

lines.speaker: Phil
lines.text: Not on purpose, he ricocheted of you and got me.

lines.speaker: Stevens
lines.text: I'm telling you, I need some smacks. I got a kid starting Dartmouth in the fall.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: (coming out of his office) Dartmouth? Who went to Dartmouth? Dartmouth sucks. Did you go to Dartmouth Bing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No sir.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: There you go. (smacks him on the butt)
The Ultimate Fighting Championship, Ross and Monica are there watching Pete.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (walking up with this huge tub-o-popcorn and drink) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God Ross, what is that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it's the Ultimate Fighting Combo. Yeah, I saved thirty cents, plus I get to keep the cup. Yay!!

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: From New York City, New York! Appearing in his Ultimate Fighting Championship debut! He's known for his confrontational business style. Ladies and Gentlemen, introducing Pete Beck-errrr!!
[Pete enters with his entourage all pumped up, and Ross and Monica are the only ones who stand up and cheer.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you, Pete!!!

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: And his opponent, from Hunnington Beach, California! He's a 300 pound street fighter, Tank Abbottttttt!!!!
The crowd goes wild, and Ross is the only one boo-ing him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (going up to the ring) Pete! Pete!! That guy's pretty huge!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Don't worry, Hoshi taught me how to use an opponent's strength and weight against him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, then that guy is in serious, serious trouble.
Pete and Monica kiss, and Monica mouths "I love you." to him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right! You go get him! Let's go!

lines.speaker: Referee
lines.text: Here we go gentlemen, here we go! (to Tank Abbott) Are you ready? (He nods, and takes out his teeth) (to Pete) Are you ready? (Pete nods, "Yes.") Let's get it on!!
They both rush each other. Tank picks Pete up and carries him over and slams him into the fence surrounding the ring.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Uh-oh.
Tank carries Pete over to the other side of the ring, and we see both Ross and Monica wince in pain.
Commercial Break
The Arena, after the fight. Monica is walking up to a defeated Pete.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! (she sits down next to him) It's me. Mon-i-ca! Can I just tell you how proud I am of you.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: It would be nice after hearing 20,000 people chant "You suck!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean I-I thought you were nuts at first, but you-you did it. And now you can just look back at this thing with no regrets.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: What, look back?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you're not gonna get going are you?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well let me ask you a question. Am I the Ultimate Fighting Champion?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, no. But...

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well I'm not gonna stop until I'm the Ultimate Fighting Champion.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That guy stood on your neck until you passed out!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Let me tell you a story. When I set out to create Moss 865, do you think it just happened overnight? No. There was Moss 1, that burnt down my Dad's garage, there was Moss 2 that would only schedule appointments in January, and 862 others that I learned from, just like I learned from this fight, never to let a guy stand on my neck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't know that already?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Look, I'm gonna get better. Okay? I promise you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just get a lot better. (pause) Fast.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, one other thing. Hoshi thinks that you being ringside may have affected my concentration.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. That-that was the problem.
Monica and Rachel's balcony, Ross and Phoebe are grilling some burgers and hot dogs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (joining them) Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! How long until Pete's fight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, about five minutes. Right now they're interviewing his opponent. Apparently he trains by going to Iran and pulling the arms off thieves.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hot dog?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Four, please. (Ross looks at her) I'm really nervous. (Ross gives her the four dogs) Thank you. (she grabs four buns, and heads back inside)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So Ross, how umm, how did it go with Bonnie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Oh! I gotta tell you, I-I wasn't expecting to like her at all, I mean I actually wasn't expecting to like anyone right now, but she's really terrific.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, that's too bad!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I'm saying I liked her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, y'know what, there are other fish in the sea.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, I think she's great. Okay? We're going out again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I hear you! Are you capable of talking about any thing else?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (joining them) Hey! Which one's my turkey burger?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, the one next to my foot. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sticking his head out the window) Hey, the fight's starting!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, we'll be right in. (to Chandler) So ah, did your boss try to slap you again today?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nine times! Okay, I had to put on lotion! But, it's gonna be okay, because as of tomorrow I'm conducting an experiment, and if all goes as planned, my butt will be smack free.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sticking his head out the window) Fight's over!
Chandler, Ross, and Phoebe all stop dead in their tracks at the news.
Chandler's office, Chandler is confronting his boss about the butt smacking thing. His boss is writing on a white board.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me, Doug? (no reaction) Hey there sports fan!!

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: (turning around) Bing! You got those numbers for me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I ah, I didn't do them.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh, you forgot?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no I just ah, didn't do them. Instead, I ah, hung out with a couple of friends and had a couple of beers so I certainly don't deserve any praise, verbal or otherwise.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Well, I got tanked myself last night. Pretty dicey drive home, Tapanzi Bridge never looked smaller. (laughs) That's okay, you're still my number one guy! (slaps him on the butt) Bing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Doug!!

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm a little bit uncomfortable with the that way you express yourself.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh, is it the swearing? I mean is it the constant swearing? Because I gotta tell ya, if it is, you can just... kiss my ass!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no. It-it's not about the swearing, it's more about ah, the way, that you ah, occasionally, concentrate, your enthusiasm on my buttock.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, and don't get me wrong, I appreciate the sentiment. It's just that I, I have a rather, sensitive posterior, and ah, besides, it's making all the other guys jealous.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Well, say no more. Y'know it takes guts to bring this up. Bing! You're okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (he starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Ha! (goes to smack him on the butt, but stops, faking Chandler out) Ahhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahhhhh! (walks out, imitating shooting himself in the head)
The street outside Central Perk, Rachel and Phoebe are talking and walking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Would you rather live in the shirt pocket of a sweaty giant, or inside his shower drain?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing Ross and Bonnie inside Central Perk) Oh my God! Phoebe look, it's Ross and that girl.
We see Ross and Bonnie laughing and having a good time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No! Look at that! (drags her away from the window) It's a line of ants! They're working as a team!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe! (goes back to the window)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking in the window) Right, oh yeah. Wow, oh, it looks like Ross is breaking up with her. Uff, I hope he lets her down easy. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on Phoebe, look at that! They are not breaking up, look at them. Okay that's, you know what that is? That is a, that is a second date, that's what that is! Look at that, she just put her hand on his thigh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! That really is nothing, she is very sexually aggressive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! (walking away from the window) Phoebe, this is all your fault! Now he loves her, he's gonna marry her, and this is all your fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You said it was okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You said she was bald!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! What-what-what-what-what?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, we can't, we just can't just let it happen! Okay, we have to do something! We have to break them up! Okay? Just go in there and like, shave her head! You owe me one bald girl!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, first of all, breathe. Second of all, I don't get it. Aren't you the one that decided that you didn't want to be with Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (quietly) Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well isn't he your friend? Don't you want him to be happy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just y'know, I didn't expect him to be this happy so soon. Ufff. Ooo-ooh! (sits down on the curb)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sits down next to her and hugs her) Oh no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, we killed them all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!
They both jump up and wipe off their butts.
A locker room, Pete is in a full upper-body cast. Monica enters, sees him, and gasps. Pete tries to turn around, and winces in pain.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: It's okay, it's not as bad as it looks, it's a precaution. Ah, I'm not supposed to move my spine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please tell me you're stopping now.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I'm fine! I'd fight tonight, if they'd let me. (stands up and starts swinging his arms) See this circle I'm marking off here? This is my zone of terror.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You are insane! You-you gotta give this up!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I can't until I'm the ultimate fighter. I will do it. I'm telling you, the day will come when children will argue over who will win a fight, me or Superman. Now, I'm not saying I could beat Superman, but y'know, kids are stupid.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sit down. All right? Please, just listen to me. You are terrible at this! Okay? You are the worst ultimate fighter ever! Ever!!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Y'know I have a torn rotator cuff, a hairline fracture in my right forearm, and a severely bruised Adam's Apple, but that really hurt.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well then, y'know what? I care about you to much to watch you hurt yourself like this. So if you have to do this, then you're gonna have to do it without me.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well if you're asking me to quit, then you're asking me to be someone I'm not. I've got to do this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then I've gotta go. Bye. (kisses him and starts to walk out)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Mon-Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Could you leave a note? 'Cause I'm on a lot of pain killers now, and I don't know if I'll remember this tomorrow. (She leaves.)
Chandler's office, he is just finishing up a meeting with his boss and the rest of his team.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: So, in conclusion, the lines all go up (points to the chart), so I'm happy. Great job team! Tomorrow at 8:30. (They start to leave) Phil! Nice job. (smacks him on the butt) Stevens! Way to go! (smacks him on the butt) Joel-burg, you maniac! I love ya! (smacks him on the butt) (Chandler walks up) Bing! Good job, couldn't have done it without ya. (he shakes his hand)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you, sir.

lines.speaker: Stevens
lines.text: (coming back in) Oh, excuse me. I forgot my briefcase y'know, by accident.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Of course, you did. Forgot something else too ya bastard! (smacks him on the butt) (to Chandler) Well, what about you? You're not feeling left out or anything are ya?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No, not at all, that's-that's ridiculous.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Everybody else got one, and you want one too. Don't you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ye-ye-yeah, yes I do!

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Now get on out of here, you! (smacks him on the butt)
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, the gang is watching Pete fight on TV. Monica is hiding in the kitchen, not watching.

lines.speaker: TV Announcer
lines.text: Pete Becker is circling the ring now. It looks like, he's just trying to feel him out. Oh, Bruiser is just...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Run! Run you crazy, rich freak!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can't watch this. (turns her eyes away)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check it out, he's winning! (to Monica) Pete's winning!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-o-o!!

lines.speaker: TV Announcer
lines.text: Uh-oh, Bruiser has Becker on the canvas and is going for his favourite area.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh! Oh! (they all recoil in horror)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, if that's his favourite area, why is he being so mean to it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, this is ironic. Of your last two boyfriends, Richard didn't want to have kids, and from the looks of it, now Pete can't.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh!! (they all start pointing at the screen)
End
